title: The Benefits of Bilingualism
description: For questions 17 – 24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The study of (0) bilingualism has expanded greatly over the past few decades. Research suggests that speaking more than one language can bring significant (17) cognitive benefits, such as improved memory and faster problem-solving. Children who grow up in (18) multilingual households tend to develop stronger (19) communication skills from an early age.||Bilingualism has also been linked to (20) professional advantages in the global job market. Employers value candidates who can work across cultures with (21) sensitivity. Additionally, speaking multiple languages increases (22) cultural understanding and fosters greater (23) tolerance between people of different backgrounds. There is also growing evidence that bilingualism may reduce the risk of mental (24) deterioration in older adults.

example.text: The study of (0) BILINGUALISM has expanded greatly over the past few decades. (BILINGUAL)

questions.explanation: The adjective 'cognitive' is formed from 'cognition' by replacing '-ition' with '-ive'.
The adjective 'multilingual' uses the prefix 'multi-' combined with 'lingual', relating to language.
The noun 'communication' is formed from 'communicate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
The adjective 'professional' is formed from 'profession' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'sensitivity' is formed from 'sensitive' by changing '-ive' to '-ivity'.
The adjective 'cultural' is formed from 'culture' by adding the suffix '-al'.
The noun 'tolerance' is formed from 'tolerate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ance'.
The noun 'deterioration' is formed from 'deteriorate' by replacing '-ate' with '-ation'.
